name: Chicken in a Clay Pot
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole chicken, about 3 pounds, cut into serving pieces
2 tablespoons each dried thyme and marjoram
recipeInstructions:
The traditional way to prepare this chicken dish from Michoacán is in a clay casserole dish covered with a sealing layer of ground corn or masa. Small bits of the corn mixture break off and add a thickening texture to the sauce. This modern version is much simpler to prepare.
1. Rub the chicken with the garlic, salt, and pepper. Refrigerate for 2 to 4 hours.
2. In a large skillet, heat the corn oil over medium heat. Sauté the chicken briefly, then transfer to a cazuela or large pot. Brown the potatoes in the same oil, remove them, and set aside.
3. Add the vinegar to the skillet and bring to a boil, scraping up browned bits from the bottom of the skillet. Strain the vinegar through a fine mesh over the chicken.
4. Add the olive oil, salt, bay leaves, thyme, marjoram, and chiles (if using) to the chicken cazuela. Bring to a boil over high heat, then cover and lower the heat to medium-high.
5. Every 10 minutes, uncover the pot and stir gently until the sauce thickens slightly. After 35 minutes, adjust the seasonings as needed and add the potatoes back into the pot. Cover and cook over low heat until the potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes.
6. Serve hot.
Makes 6 servings.
